basic_details.job_title: Haryana High Court Clerk Vacancy 2026 Notification Out - Apply Online for 1265 Posts
basic_details.organization_name: Society for Centralized Recruitment of Staff in Subordinate Courts (S.S.S.C.) under High Court of Punjab and Haryana, Chandigarh
basic_details.department: District and Sessions Courts across the State of Haryana
basic_details.total_vacancies: 1265 (1015 existing as on 31.12.2025 + 250 anticipated up to 30.06.2026)
basic_details.application_mode: Online only at www.sssc.gov.in under "Clerk Subordinate Courts of Haryana"
basic_details.short_information: Haryana High Court is recruiting 1265 Clerk posts for Subordinate Courts of Haryana with Bachelor's degree qualification (BA/BSc or equivalent) and computer proficiency, last date 09 July 2026.

important_dates.qualification cut-off date: 23-06-2026 (closing date for submission of online applications)
important_dates.ews certificate validity required: Valid for FY 2026-2027, based on gross annual income for FY 2025-2026, issued on or before 23-06-2026
important_dates.demo / mock test availability: 15 days prior to date of examination (on www.sssc.gov.in)
important_dates.exam_date: To be notified on www.sssc.gov.in (exam can be held anywhere in Northern India)
important_dates.document verification / interaction date: To be notified after written exam and CPT results

application_fee.details: Fee is non-refundable and must be paid online only. The Online Application Form will be submitted only after payment of fees. Application fee for "Others" (Transgender) category is applicable as per their category in the fee table below.

age_limit.details: Age is calculated as on 01.01.2026. The upper age limit for any candidate (except Ex-servicemen) shall not exceed 52 years even after availing all applicable age relaxations combined. Reservation benefits of age relaxation are only for bonafide residents of Haryana. | DON'T MISSRRB 6565 Technician Online Form 2026 (Short Notice)6,565 Posts • Technician • RRB29 Jul 20266,565 postsOSSSC 5989 Nursing Officer Online Form 20265,989 Vacancies • Nursing Officer13 Jul 20265,989 postsGSRTC 4599 Driver Online Form 20264,599 Openings • GSRTC05 Jul 20264,599 postsGSRTC 4318 Conductor Online Form 20264,318 Posts • GSRTC05 Jul 20264,318 postsKSP Civil Police Constable Online Form 20263,991 Vacancies • Civil Police Constable • KSP03 Jul 20263,991 postsMPSC 2619 Group C Online Form 20262,619 Openings • Group C17 Jul 20262,619 posts

qualification.details: Candidates must fulfill all eligibility conditions as on the closing date of application (09-07-2026). All particulars including category and sub-category once filled by the applicant in the Online Application Form shall be sacrosanct and no change shall be permitted at any stage of the recruitment process.
qualification.domicile: As per Haryana Subordinate Courts Establishment (Recruitment and General Conditions of Service) Rules, 1997. Reservation benefits only for bonafide Haryana residents with valid Haryana Government-issued certificates.
qualification.education_qualification: Bachelor of Arts (BA) or Bachelor of Science (BSc) or equivalent degree from a recognized university. AND proficiency in computers (Word Processing and Spread Sheets).

vacancy_details.Category: Scheduled Castes of Haryana - Deprived SC (DSC) (incl. 37 backlog - 2nd time)
Scheduled Castes of Haryana - Other SC (OSC) (incl. 33 backlog - 2nd time)
Backward Classes (A) (BC-A) of Haryana (incl. 47 backlog - 2nd time; 1 backlog in anticipated - 2nd time)
Backward Classes (B) (BC-B) of Haryana (incl. 32 backlog - 2nd time)
PwBD - HH (Hard of Hearing) (incl. 7 backlog - 2nd time)
PwBD - OA, BA, OL, BL, OAL, LC, Dw, AAV (incl. 3 backlog - 2nd time)
PwBD - SLD, MI & MD involving (a to d)
ESM - Deprived SC (incl. 9 backlog: 8 - 2nd time, 1 - 3rd time)
OA, BA, OL, BL, OAL, LC, Dw, AAV
vacancy_details.Description: One Arm, Both Arms, One Leg, Both Legs, One Arm and One Leg, Leprosy Cured, Dwarfism, Acid Attack Victims
Multiple Disabilities involving any combination of categories (a) to (d) above

category_wise_vacancy.Description: Multiple Disabilities involving any combination of categories (a) to (d) above

salary_details.pay_scale: As per latest rules and instructions of Government of Haryana duly adopted for Subordinate Courts of Haryana

selection_process: Sr. No. Subject Type No. of Questions Maximum Marks Qualifying Marks

1 General Knowledge Objective (MCQ) 50 questions × 1 mark each 50 33% in each subject individually; AND minimum 40% aggregate of both subjects. English Composition (Subjective) checked only for candidates who score 33%+ in General Knowledge.
2 English Composition Objective (MCQ) 50 questions × 1 mark each 20
Subjective (typed on computer) i. Essay (150 words) 10
ii. Letter 05
iii. Precis 05
iv. Translation from Hindi to English 10
Total 100 Minimum 40% aggregate
Part Test Duration Marks Qualifying Marks
Part I Spread Sheet Test 10 minutes 10 marks 40% or more (4 or more marks)

Part II Computer Typing Test in English (Word Processing) Stipulated time Qualifying 30 WPM (Words Per Minute). Formula: (Words Typed − Mistakes) ÷ 10. Part II checked only for those who qualify Part I.

Selection is through a 3-stage process: Online Written Examination (Computer Based Test) → Computer Proficiency Test (qualifying) → Checking of Original Testimonials / Interaction. The final Select List is prepared strictly on the basis of merit in the Written Examination (CBT) only. Computer Proficiency Test marks are NOT counted in the final merit. Success in examination confers no right of appointment unless the Competent Authority is satisfied after enquiry.
Stage 1 - Online Written Examination / Computer Based Test (CBT)

The CBT is conducted online (Computer Based Test format) in the following subjects. The exam may be held on different dates for different sets of candidates (multiple shifts). Normalization of objective scores using Standard Deviation Method will be applied if exam is held in multiple shifts.

Negative Marking: 1/4th mark deducted for every wrong answer in MCQ portion. Questions not attempted receive no credit or penalty. Subjective English Composition will be typed on computer within the stipulated time.
Tie-break Rule: If two or more candidates obtain same marks
the elder in age is ranked higher. If age is also same
candidates are placed alphabetically (A to Z) in the merit list.

Answer Key & Objections: Provisional Answer Key will be uploaded on www.sssc.gov.in after CBT. Candidates can raise objections at Rs. 25/- per objection (non-refundable). Cross-objections against proposed changes in provisional answer key are also allowed. Objections/cross-objections must include proposed correct answer and supporting reasons with documentary proof.
Stage 2 - Computer Proficiency Test (CPT) - Qualifying Nature Only
After CBT results
a category-wise determined number of candidates will be called for CPT. CPT marks are NOT counted in final merit - it is purely qualifying in nature.
PwBD Exemption from CPT: PwBD with Both Arms Affected (BA) can apply for full exemption from CPT with Disability Certificate. PwBD as defined under Section 2(r) of RPwD Act

2016 can apply for CPT exemption with Disability Certificate + Medical Certificate from CMO/Civil Surgeon/Medical Superintendent of a Government healthcare institution certifying physical limitation to type at 30 WPM. PwBD under Section 2(s) of RPwD Act can apply for exemption with Medical Certificate only. All such applications must be submitted within 15 days of declaration of CBT result.
Stage 3 - Checking of Original Testimonials / Interaction

Candidates who qualify both CBT and CPT (category-wise as decided by the High Court) will be called for checking of original testimonials/interaction. No TA/DA shall be paid for appearing at any stage of the recruitment process including this stage. The Select List will be prepared strictly on the basis of CBT merit.
how_to_apply: Candidates must apply in online mode only at www.sssc.gov.in under the head "Clerk Subordinate Courts of Haryana". No other means/mode of submission of application will be accepted. The online application form is submitted only after payment of fees. All applicants (including reserved category) can submit only one application per category - if more than one application is submitted, only the latest application will be considered.
Before starting the Online Application Form, keep the following ready:
Personal details (Name, Date of Birth, Category, Sub-category etc.)
Valid and active Email ID (keep unchanged throughout the process - do not use email of unknown persons)
Valid and active mobile number (keep unchanged throughout the process)
Online payment facility - Internet Banking / Debit Card / Credit Card / UPI
Scanned recent passport-size photograph (not more than one month old, against white background)
Scanned signature
Computer/device with inbuilt or attached webcam (for real-time photograph capture during application)
Documents to be Uploaded in Online Application Form (before 09-07-2026):
Real-time photograph captured via webcam/camera at time of filling form
Scanned recent passport-size photograph (not more than one month old)
Scanned signature
All other requisite documents as specified in the Online Application Form
Documents to be Produced at Checking of Original Testimonials / Interaction:
Printout of Online Application Form (retain 5 copies of the photograph uploaded in the application)
E-Admit Card (downloaded and printed from www.sssc.gov.in, duly self-attested / attested by a Gazetted Officer)
Date of Birth certificate / Matriculation certificate
Bachelor's degree certificate and mark sheets
Matriculation mark sheet showing Hindi/Sanskrit as a subject (or +2/BA/MA mark sheet showing Hindi)
Computer proficiency proof
Caste certificate (in prescribed format, issued by competent authority of Haryana Government, valid as on 23-06-2026) - for SC/DSC/OSC/BC-A/BC-B/EWS candidates
BC category undertaking that no change has occurred in their status and they do not fall in Creamy Layer up to 23-06-2026
EWS Income & Assets Certificate (valid for FY 2026-2027, based on FY 2025-2026 income, issued on or before 23-06-2026, on prescribed Haryana Government proforma)
Disability Certificate (for PwBD candidates - in prescribed format as per RPwD Act, 2016)
ESM Identity Card from Zila Sainik Board / Discharge Certificate or Book
ESM Family Member Eligibility Certificate from Zila Sainik Board (valid as on 23-06-2026)
Sports Certificate (for ESP candidates - as per applicable Haryana Government orders)
Freedom Fighter Certificate from competent authority (for Freedom Fighter / children / grandchildren)
No Objection Certificate (for in-service Government employees - from Head of Office/Department)
Experience Certificate of equivalent post from Appointing Authority (for adhoc/contract/daily wages employees availing age relaxation)
Any other documents in support of eligibility/category claimed in application

For Re-checking of Answer Sheets (if required after CBT result): Written request to Member Secretary, S.S.S.C., High Court of Punjab and Haryana, Chandigarh, along with fee of Rs. 500/- per answer sheet (Indian Postal Order payable to S.S.S.C.), within 30 days of declaration of final result. Re-evaluation is not allowed - only re-checking for totalling errors.
Apply Online at: www.sssc.gov.in → Clerk Subordinate Courts of Haryana → Registration Form → Application Form → Fee Payment

For Technical queries: Helpline No. +91 99866-38762 (Working days, 09:30 AM – 05:00 PM). For terms and conditions queries: Phone 0172-2722012 / 9115898394 (Working days, 09:30 AM – 05:00 PM). All corrigenda/notices will be notified on www.sssc.gov.in only - newspaper advertisements, if any, are secondary; the SSSC website content prevails.
important_instructions: All applicants irrespective of their category (General or reserved) can submit only one application per category. If more than one application is submitted, only the latest application will be considered. All particulars including category and sub-category once filled in the Online Application Form are sacrosanct - no change shall be permitted at any stage of the recruitment process. Fill all details after taking due care and precaution. Reservation benefits (age, fee, category) are only for bonafide residents of Haryana with valid Haryana Government-issued certificates valid as on 09-07-2026. Candidates from other states' reserved categories must apply under General category. The photograph pasted on the E-Admit Card must be the same as uploaded in the Online Application Form. Mismatch will result in denial of entry at the examination centre. Facial appearance at the time of examination must match the photographs in the application and E-Admit Card - failing which candidature can be rejected at any stage. No TA/DA shall be paid for appearing in any stage of the selection process. No mobile phone, electronic device, watch, jewellery, or baggage material is allowed inside the examination centre. The decision of the High Court in all matters relating to acceptance/rejection of application, eligibility/suitability, mode/criteria for selection shall be final and binding. The High Court may shortlist candidates at any stage based on any criteria. Candidates are advised to apply online well before the closing date to avoid last-minute website congestion. The cut-off date is sacrosanct - no extension will be granted under any circumstance, and requests by post/fax/email/by hand will not be entertained. Mere satisfying eligibility criteria does not entitle an applicant to be called for examination. The High Court reserves the right to alter/modify or change any of the terms and conditions including selection criteria. Concealing any material fact or furnishing false information can result in rejection by the High Court at any stage. Canvassing in any form will entail cancellation of candidature. The candidature at all stages is purely provisional - it can be cancelled at any point if the applicant is found not meeting eligibility conditions, even after completion of the selection process. Success in the examination confers no right of appointment unless the Competent Authority is satisfied that the candidate is suitable in all respects for appointment to the service. All communications to SSSC must invariably contain: Advertisement No. & Post Name, Registration ID, Roll No. (if received), Full Name in Block Letters, Registered Email ID, and Correspondence Address as given in the application form. In case of any discrepancy between the advertisement in newspapers and the SSSC website content, www.sssc.gov.in shall prevail. All notices, results, exam dates, and corrigendum will be notified on www.sssc.gov.in only - candidates must check the website and their email (inbox as well as spam box) regularly.

faq.question: Q1. What is the total number of vacancies in Punjab and Haryana High Court Clerk Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The High Court of Punjab and Haryana has announced a total of 1265 Clerk vacancies under the Punjab and Haryana High Court Clerk Recruitment 2026. This includes 1015 existing posts and 250 anticipated vacancies up to June 2026. The number of vacancies may change depending on administrative requirements at the time of final selection

faq.question: Q2. What is the eligibility criteria for Haryana SSSC Clerk 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates must have a Bachelor’s degree (BA, BSc, or equivalent) from a recognized university along with basic computer proficiency in Word Processing and Spreadsheets. Additionally, applicants must have studied Hindi or Sanskrit at Matric level or higher. All qualifications must be completed before 09 July 2026.

faq.question: Q3. What is the age limit for Punjab and Haryana High Court Clerk Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The minimum age is 18 years and the maximum age is 42 years for General category candidates as on 01 January 2026. Reserved categories like SC, BC, PwBD, and others are eligible for age relaxation as per Haryana government rules. However, the upper age limit cannot exceed 52 years after all relaxations.

faq.question: Q4. What is the last date to apply for Punjab and Haryana High Court Clerk Vacancy 2026?
faq.answer: The online application process will start on 26 May 2026 at 4:00 PM and will remain open until 09 July 2026 at 4:00 PM. Candidates are advised to complete their application well before the deadline to avoid last-minute technical issues. Late submissions will not be accepted under any circumstances.

faq.question: Q5. What is the selection process for Haryana High Court Clerk Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The selection process consists of three stages: Computer Based Test (CBT), Computer Proficiency Test (CPT), and document verification. The final merit list is prepared based on marks obtained in the CBT only. The CPT is qualifying in nature and must be cleared to proceed further.

faq.question: Q6. What is the exam pattern of Punjab and Haryana High Court Clerk CBT 2026?
faq.answer: The CBT includes General Knowledge and English Composition sections with both objective and subjective questions. The exam carries 100 marks with a duration of 2 hours, and there is negative marking of 1/4 for incorrect answers. Candidates must score at least 33% in each section and 40% overall to qualify.

faq.question: Q7. Is there any application fee for Haryana Clerk Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Yes, the application fee varies based on category and gender. General category males need to pay Rs. 1200, while females pay Rs. 875. Haryana reserved categories pay Rs. 710, and PwBD candidates are exempted from paying any fee.

faq.question: Q8. Who can apply for Punjab and Haryana High Court Clerk posts under reserved categories?
faq.answer: Only bonafide residents of Haryana are eligible to claim reservation benefits under SC, BC, EWS, ESM, and other categories. Candidates from other states must apply under the General category even if they belong to a reserved category. Valid and updated certificates are mandatory at the time of application.

faq.question: Q9. What is the salary of Haryana High Court Clerk 2026?
faq.answer: The pay scale for Clerk posts will be as per the latest rules and instructions issued by the Government of Haryana. Along with basic pay, selected candidates will also receive allowances and benefits applicable to state government employees. The job is permanent and offers stable career growth.

faq.question: Q10. How to apply online for Punjab and Haryana High Court Clerk Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates must apply online through the official website sssc.gov.in by completing registration, filling the application form, uploading documents, and paying the fee. It is important to keep scanned documents, a valid email ID, and a working mobile number ready before applying. The application is considered complete only after successful fee payment.
